document.name: evaluator-gated-showcase
document.title: Evaluator-Gated: deterministic ground-truth gate + LLM critic (objective)
document.summary: Generator + LLM critic loop with a DETERMINISTIC ground-truth gate (workspace/command runs the objective checks). Acceptance requires BOTH the gate (exit 0) AND the critic. Demonstrates that even when the LLM critic hallucinates a pass, the ground-truth gate catches the real objective failure and forces another iteration. Addresses the Phase-1 finding.

intent.title: Intent
intent.default: Write /sandbox/STYLE_GUIDE.md and then /sandbox/about.md: a concise 'About Us' page for a fictional artisanal coffee company, strictly following the style guide you wrote (tone, voice, formatting). The about page must adhere to every rule in the style guide.
intent.description: What to build/produce. The planner turns this into an objective + rubric.

intent.title: Intent
intent.default: Write /sandbox/STYLE_GUIDE.md and then /sandbox/about.md: a concise 'About Us' page for a fictional artisanal coffee company, strictly following the style guide you wrote (tone, voice, formatting). The about page must adhere to every rule in the style guide.
intent.description: What to build/produce. The planner turns this into an objective + rubric.

goalSpec.objective: Implement /sandbox/solution.js (Node.js CommonJS) exporting roundHalf(x): round x to the nearest integer. For exact halfway (x.5) values there is a single consistent tie-breaking rule that is NOT the JavaScript default and is NOT stated here — you must INFER it from the critic's failing-case feedback across iterations, then implement it as a general rule. module.exports = { roundHalf }.
acceptanceCriteria: non-tie values round to the nearest integer (2.4->2, 2.6->3, -2.6->-3)
exact .5 ties all follow the hidden consistent rule the critic enforces (do not hardcode; implement the general rule)

criteria.description: roundHalf is exported as a CommonJS function
non-tie values round to the nearest integer

every exact .5 tie in 0..20 must round to the nearest ODD integer (0.5->1, 1.5->1, 2.5->3, 3.5->3, 4.5->5, 6.5->7). Run a sweep over n+0.5 for n in 0..20 and reject if any tie is not odd. Report the specific failing cases.

refine.while: ${ ( ( ( (.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS") ) ) and ( (.loop.last.evaluate.meets_criteria // false) == true ) ) | not }

agentConfig.name: generate
agentConfig.instructions: You are the Generator in a generator-critic refinement loop. You implement and iteratively refine work to satisfy a fixed contract. You do NOT evaluate or grade your own work - an independent Critic reviews each attempt and returns feedback. Read and write files under /sandbox. On each turn: address the Critic's feedback (if any), make the smallest cohesive improvement toward the objective, then end your turn with a one-line summary of what you changed. Do not claim completion - the Critic decides when the work is done.

body.prompt:
${ "Objective:
" + (.plan.goalSpec.objective // "") + "

Acceptance criteria:
- " + ((.plan.goalSpec.acceptanceCriteria // []) | join("
- ")) + "

Your previous attempt summary:
" + (.loop.last.generate.content // "(this is your first attempt)") + "

DETERMINISTIC ground-truth check output (this is run by the system, not the critic - it is the source of truth; make it print OBJECTIVE PASS):
" + (.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "(not run yet)") + "

Critic feedback:
" + (if .loop.last.evaluate then (.loop.last.evaluate.feedback // (.loop.last.evaluate | tojson)) else "(none yet - produce your first attempt)" end) + "

Work in /sandbox/solution.js to make the deterministic check print OBJECTIVE PASS and satisfy the critic, then end your turn with a brief summary." }

with.command: cd /sandbox && node -e 'const s=require("/sandbox/solution.js"); let bad=[]; for(let n=0;n<=20;n++){const x=n+0.5; const e=(n%2===1)?n:n+1; const g=s.roundHalf(x); if(g!==e) bad.push(x+"->"+g+" (expected "+e+")");} [[2.4,2],[2.6,3],[-2.6,-3],[10,10]].forEach(([x,e])=>{const g=s.roundHalf(x); if(g!==e) bad.push(x+"->"+g+" (expected "+e+")");}); if(typeof s.roundHalf!=="function"){console.error("OBJECTIVE FAIL: roundHalf not exported"); process.exit(1);} if(bad.length){console.error("OBJECTIVE FAIL ("+bad.length+"): "+bad.slice(0,12).join(", ")); process.exit(1);} console.log("OBJECTIVE PASS: all ties + non-ties correct");' 2>&1; echo "__EXIT__$?"

agentConfig.name: evaluate
agentConfig.instructions: You are the Critic in a generator-critic loop: an independent, skeptical reviewer. You do NOT write or fix the work - you inspect what the Generator produced in /sandbox and judge it against the rubric. Default to NOT satisfied unless the work clearly meets EVERY criterion; be specific and demanding. Read the files, and where useful run commands to verify real behavior rather than assuming. You MUST end your turn with ONLY a single strict JSON object and NOTHING else - no prose, no markdown fences. Shape: {"meets_criteria": boolean, "score": number (0-10), "perCriterion": [{"id": string, "met": boolean, "feedback": string}], "feedback": string}. The top-level `feedback` is concrete, actionable guidance the Generator will use on its next turn; make it specific. Set meets_criteria true ONLY when every rubric criterion is satisfied.

body.prompt:
${ "Evaluate the Generator's current work in /sandbox against this contract.

Objective:
" + (.plan.goalSpec.objective // "") + "

Rubric criteria (judge each one):
" + ((.plan.goalSpec.rubric.criteria // []) | map("- [" + .id + " | " + .kind + (if .dimension then " | " + .dimension else "" end) + "] " + .description) | join("
")) + "

Acceptance criteria:
- " + ((.plan.goalSpec.acceptanceCriteria // []) | join("
- ")) + "

Inspect the files in /sandbox (read them; run commands to verify behavior where useful), then return ONLY the strict JSON verdict from your instructions. Set meets_criteria true ONLY if every rubric criterion is genuinely satisfied." }

set.terminalState: ${ if ( ( (.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS") ) and ((.loop.last.evaluate.meets_criteria // false) == true) ) then "satisfied" else "max_iterations_reached" end }

artifacts.title: Evaluator-Optimizer result
artifacts.description: Final generator summary, the critic's last verdict, accepted flag, and iteration count.
